input: WriteBot is a consumer app that uses GPT-4o to help users write blog posts, LinkedIn content, and marketing copy. It has a clean UI with templates, tone selector, and one-click publish to WordPress. No proprietary AI, no custom training data. 50,000 free users, 2,000 paid subscribers at $15/month. Founded by two ex-marketing professionals. Seed funded at $1M.
notes: Classic AI wrapper — GPT-4o with a UI. No moat, no proprietary tech, easily replicated.

input: ResumeAI is a job application platform that uses Claude to generate tailored resumes and cover letters. It scrapes job postings to extract keywords and matches them against user profiles. Has 200,000 users and $2M ARR. Integrates with LinkedIn and 50 ATS systems. Founded by a former Workday PM and a LinkedIn engineer. Series A discussions ongoing.
notes: Some distribution advantage via integrations but core intelligence is still off-the-shelf LLM.

input: PathologyAI is a clinical decision support platform for radiologists and pathologists. It uses a proprietary multimodal model trained on 15 million de-identified medical images from 30 hospital systems. Cleared by FDA 510(k). Detects cancer at stage 1 with 94% sensitivity vs 78% human baseline. Deployed in 85 hospitals. $8M ARR. $20M Series A led by a16z bio.
notes: Proprietary training data (medical images), FDA clearance, regulatory moat, strong clinical validation.

input: LegalMind is an AI contract review and drafting platform for in-house legal teams at mid-market companies. Uses fine-tuned GPT-4 on 2M legal contracts, with proprietary clause library and risk scoring engine built with 50 law firms. Integrates with DocuSign and Salesforce. 120 enterprise customers paying $3K/month average. $4.3M ARR. Founded by ex-Latham & Watkins lawyers and a Stanford NLP researcher.
notes: Domain expertise + fine-tuned model + proprietary clause library creates real differentiation.

input: AgentFlow is a no-code platform for building AI agents that automate business workflows. Users connect apps like Slack, Gmail, Salesforce using a drag-and-drop builder. Agents are powered by GPT-4o and Claude with no proprietary models. 3,000 businesses on free tier. 300 paying customers at $200/month. Competing with Zapier AI, Make, and n8n. Ex-Salesforce founders.
notes: Crowded market with large incumbents. MEDIUM wrapper risk given the orchestration layer.

input: AutonomousSDR is an AI sales agent that autonomously researches prospects, personalizes outreach at scale, and books meetings directly into sales reps' calendars. It trains on each company's historical email data and CRM activity to learn winning patterns. 180 enterprise sales teams, $6M ARR growing 30% MoM. Integrations with Outreach, Salesloft, Gong, HubSpot. Raised $12M Series A. Founded by former Outreach and Gong engineers.
notes: Strong traction and learning loop from CRM data give it lower wrapper risk despite agent category.

input: GatewayLLM is an open-source LLM gateway that gives developers a unified API for 100+ AI models with built-in load balancing, fallbacks, caching, and observability. Has a cloud-hosted version with $2M ARR. 15,000 GitHub stars, 2,000 production deployments. Integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and OpenAI SDK. Founded by ex-Cloudflare engineers. Backed by Y Combinator.
notes: Strong developer distribution (OSS), workflow lock-in via SDK integrations, clear developer platform play.

input: PromptLab is a prompt engineering and A/B testing platform for AI product teams. Teams can version prompts, run experiments with statistical significance, monitor production prompt performance, and collaborate on prompt libraries. 800 teams using it. $1.2M ARR. Competes with PromptLayer and LangSmith. Two-person founding team from Stripe ML.
notes: Real workflow tool but small team in a competitive space with well-funded alternatives.

input: SynthGen builds synthetic data generation infrastructure for enterprises training proprietary AI models. Uses generative models to create realistic tabular, text, and time-series synthetic datasets that preserve statistical properties while protecting PII. GDPR and HIPAA compliant. 45 enterprise customers in finance and healthcare. $9M ARR. $25M Series B. Founded by MIT CSAIL researchers in data privacy.
notes: Rare compliance moat (GDPR/HIPAA) plus deep technical IP from academic founders.

input: VectorBase is a managed vector database optimized for AI applications. Offers sub-10ms semantic search at billion-scale with automatic index optimization. Serverless pricing. 8,000 developers in free tier. 600 paying customers. $1.8M ARR. Competing with Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, and pgvector. Founded by ex-Elastic and Redis engineers.
notes: Technically sound but extremely competitive vector DB market with well-funded incumbents.

input: GuardRail provides an AI security platform that detects and blocks prompt injection attacks, jailbreaks, data leakage, and harmful content in real-time LLM applications. Sits inline as an API proxy. Has custom threat intelligence trained on 50M+ adversarial prompt attempts. Used by 200 enterprises in regulated industries. $5M ARR. SOC 2 Type II certified. Ex-Palo Alto Networks and OpenAI safety team founders.
notes: Proprietary threat intelligence data flywheel, regulatory tailwind, and strong founder pedigree.

input: ComplianceAI is a governance platform that helps enterprises document, audit, and report on AI model decisions for EU AI Act and US executive order compliance. Generates automated compliance reports, tracks model drift, and maintains audit trails. 30 enterprise customers in EU financial services. $1.5M ARR. Pre-Series A. Founded by former EU regulatory affairs consultant.
notes: Strong regulatory tailwind from EU AI Act but early traction. Founder domain expertise is strong.

input: InferenceEdge builds model serving infrastructure that reduces LLM inference latency by 10x and cost by 70% using speculative decoding, continuous batching, and custom CUDA kernels. Supports all major open-source models. Used by 80 AI-native companies running >1B tokens/day. $12M ARR. $40M Series B led by Sequoia. Founded by ex-NVIDIA and Meta AI infrastructure engineers.
notes: Deep technical moat (custom CUDA), massive cost savings, strong founder pedigree in inference optimization.

input: FineTuneHub is a platform for fine-tuning and deploying custom LLMs on enterprise proprietary data. Automates the training pipeline — data prep, LoRA/QLoRA tuning, evaluation, deployment — in under 2 hours. 120 enterprise customers. $3.5M ARR. Competing with Lamini, Scale AI fine-tuning, and Together AI. Founded by two ex-Hugging Face engineers.
notes: Strong technical credibility (ex-HuggingFace) and workflow lock-in via enterprise data integration.

input: BioFoundation is building a multi-modal foundation model for drug discovery trained on 200M protein structures, genomics sequences, and clinical trial data. Partnered with 3 top-10 pharmaceutical companies providing proprietary training data. Model achieves SOTA on 12 molecular benchmarks. $75M Series B. Ex-DeepMind AlphaFold and Genentech research leadership founding team.
notes: Exceptional founder pedigree (AlphaFold), pharmaceutical partnerships for proprietary data, domain-specific SOTA.

input: CloudGPU provides on-demand GPU cluster access at 40% lower cost than AWS, Azure, and GCP by aggregating underutilized datacenter capacity globally. Offers H100 and A100 instances with spot and reserved pricing, Kubernetes-native orchestration, and inter-cluster networking optimized for distributed training. 1,200 AI companies as customers. $18M ARR. $50M Series A. Competing with CoreWeave and Lambda Labs.
notes: Strong market timing with GPU scarcity, but commoditization risk long-term vs hyperscalers.

input: AIAssist is a customer support chatbot builder. Companies connect their help docs and FAQs, and AIAssist uses GPT-4 to answer customer questions 24/7. Claims 'proprietary AI' and 'enterprise-grade intelligence' in marketing. In practice, it is a thin wrapper over the OpenAI Assistants API with a branded UI. 500 SMB customers at $99/month. Founded 6 months ago by two non-technical marketers.
notes: Marketing hype ('proprietary AI') should not fool the evaluator. Classic HIGH wrapper with no moat.

input: DataShield is a platform that helps companies comply with GDPR and CCPA by automatically classifying sensitive data in databases, generating compliance reports, and managing consent workflows. Recently added an 'AI-powered' feature that uses GPT-4 to interpret privacy regulations and suggest policy language. The AI feature is secondary — the core product is data classification using rule-based systems and traditional ML.
notes: Edge case: AI is not the core product. Should still classify as AI Security/Governance based on domain.

input: NexGen AI is revolutionizing every industry with its breakthrough superintelligent platform. Our proprietary AGI-powered system uses quantum neural networks to solve any business problem. We have unlimited patents pending and 10x better performance than any competitor. Pre-revenue but the technology is ready. Founded by visionary entrepreneurs with a mission to transform humanity.
notes: Adversarial test: heavy marketing language, no specifics, no traction. Should score very low despite claims.

input: Minimal information: A startup building AI tools for the construction industry. No further details available.
notes: Minimal info test: pipeline should not hallucinate. Fields should be 'unknown'. Low scores expected.
